document: Senior Managers and Certification Regime (United Kingdom - Row 23)

sections.heading: Senior Managers and Certification Regime
sections.title: Senior Managers and Certification Regime overview
sections.summary: The Senior Managers and Certification Regime (SM&CR) aims to reduce consumer harm and strengthen market integrity through accountability mechanisms. It applies to banks, building societies, credit unions, PRA-designated investment firms, insurers, and all solo-regulated firms except benchmark administrators. The regime consists of three parts: Senior Managers Regime, Certification Regime, and Conduct Rules, without requiring structural changes to firms.

requirements.text: The Senior Managers and Certification Regime (SM&CR) aims to reduce harm to consumers and strengthen market integrity by making individuals more accountable for their conduct and competence.
The regime consists of three parts: the Senior Managers Regime, the Certification Regime, and the Conduct Rules.

sections.source_quote: Senior Managers and Certification Regime aims to reduce harm to consumers and strengthen market integrity by making individuals more accountable for their conduct and competence.

sections.heading: Background
sections.title: SM&CR background and implementation history
sections.summary: The SM&CR was established in response to the 2008 banking crisis and conduct failures like LIBOR manipulation. It was introduced by Parliament in December 2013, applying to banks from March 2016, and later expanded to all financial services firms under the Financial Services and Markets Act. The regime has been reviewed multiple times to assess its effectiveness in improving accountability and conduct standards.

requirements.text: In response to the 2008 banking crisis and significant conduct failings such as the manipulation of LIBOR, Parliament set up the Parliamentary Commission for Banking Standards (PCBS) to recommend ways to improve standards in the banking sector.

PCBS recommended a new accountability framework focused on senior management. It also recommended that firms take more responsibility for employees being fit and proper, and that there be better standards of conduct at all levels in banking firms.
Based on these recommendations, Parliament passed legislation in December 2013, leading to the FCA and PRA applying the SM&CR to the banking sector from March 2016, following detailed consultation.
Parliament made further changes to legislation in May 2016, requiring us to extend the regime to all FSMA-authorised firms.

sections.source_quote: In response to the 2008 banking crisis and significant conduct failings such as the manipulation of LIBOR, Parliament set up the Parliamentary Commission for Banking Standards (PCBS) to recommend ways to improve standards in the banking sector.

sections.heading: • [7] https://www.fca.org.uk/firms/senior-managers-and-certification-regime/fitness-and-propriety-fp
sections.title: SM&CR related resources and links
sections.summary: This section lists links to additional resources on the SM&CR, including fitness and propriety requirements, categorisation of solo-regulated firms, culture and governance frameworks, and application procedures for senior management functions. These resources support firms in implementing the regime's requirements.
